title: androsace rotundifolia var. fragilis
intro: Androsace rotundifolia var. fragilis, also known as Fragile Androsace, is a rare and endangered alpine plant species native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and rounded leaves. Traditionally, Fragile Androsace has been used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of various infections. Fragile Androsace can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, although its use in modern herbal medicine is largely anecdotal and requires further scientific research.
